success_surface: Cell-Level Formula Manipulation Across Multiple Rows
effective_behaviors: Inspect target cells before modification to understand existing structure
Apply formulas to entire required range with adjusted row references
Use formula strings instead of evaluated values for dynamic calculations
Verify changes after execution by comparing formulas and source values
Select appropriate library (openpyxl) for formula preservation over pandas
Save output to new path without modifying original source file
evidence: Agent loaded workbook and inspected D4 formula before making changes: print(f'Cell D4 formula: {ws[D4].value}')
Applied formula across D4:D15 range with dynamic reference adjustment: adjusted_formula = new_formula.replace('C4', f'C{row}')
Wrote formula as raw string starting with equals sign: ws[cell_ref].value = adjusted_formula
Verified results post-save by printing both formula and corresponding input values
Used openpyxl.load_workbook() and wb.save() for formula-capable operations
Saved to output_path variable rather than modifying original file location

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect the current state of target cells before making formula or data modifications.
memory_items.generalization: Query existing cell values/formulas in the affected range before overwriting to ensure understanding of current logic and avoid unintended replacements.

memory_items.title: Multi-Row Formula Scaling
memory_items.lesson: When applying formulas across multiple rows, adjust cell references dynamically for each target cell.
memory_items.generalization: Create a template formula and programmatically replace row/column placeholders before assigning to each cell in the target range.

memory_items.title: Formula String Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Write Excel formulas as strings beginning with '=' to maintain dynamic calculation capabilities.
memory_items.generalization: Never pre-evaluate formulas; always store the formula expression itself so it recalculates based on dependent cells.

memory_items.title: Post-Execution Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving changes, verify a sample of target cells show expected formulas and values.
memory_items.generalization: Compare written formulas against input cell values for several rows to confirm logic applied correctly across the range.

memory_items.title: Library Selection for Formulas
memory_items.lesson: Choose openpyxl over pandas when working with Excel formulas to preserve calculation chains.
memory_items.generalization: Use openpyxl for any operation requiring formula retention; use pandas only for pure data transformation where formulas aren't needed.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Pre-Modification Inspection**: Query existing cell values and formulas in the target range before overwriting to understand current logic and avoid unintended replacements.
- **Dynamic Reference Adjustment**: When applying formulas across multiple rows or columns, create a template formula and programmatically replace row/column placeholders before assignment.
- **Formula String Retention**: Always write formulas as raw strings starting with '=' to maintain dynamic recalculation; never evaluate formulas before writing.
- **Verification Protocol**: After saving changes, spot-check both the formula strings and corresponding calculated values to confirm logic applied correctly across the target range.
- **Library Matching**: Select openpyxl when formulas must be preserved; pandas strips formula expressions during read/write cycles.
